name: Page Barnes
text: I remember meeting Izzy as a small child when your family moved on to the next street. They were so cute, bright and energetic. Through the years, I particularly enjoyed watching Izzy on stage where they were so fearless and hilarious — characteristics that carried over into real life. Even as their illness progressed, Izzy always maintained a spark of light and energy. Their memory will live on for all of us who knew them. Wishing comfort to you as you face this immeasurable loss.

name: Ashley Tran
text: I've known Izzy since preschool. I remember all the great times together at that age - the playdates, playing dress-up, and all the different games we would play together. You will be dearly missed. IZZY4EVER 🤍

name: Amor
text:
Isaac touched so many lives. It’s hard to put into words what a shining light he was. A strong, inspiring person who always encouraged me to follow my creative endeavors & continues to guide them. 
Even when we first met at LMS and I knew nobody, he showed me a sense of kindness and understanding without question and I’ll forever treasure the loving friend he was.
There are so many movies, songs, videos we shared that I’ll always see a part of him in. Whether it was midday trips to the donut shop, watching cult classics after school, weaving stories over Dungeons & Dragons together, or joking around backstage at poetry jams, he’s a constant presence in my best memories. It was amazing to be invited to tag along to the release party for Interfectorem and get to see his work celebrated, as well as stay up late chatting and goofing off the whole way to Mountain View and back.
I’ll always hold close his contributions to the ACLC poetry books, the heartfelt graduation video he made for us, the games & podcasts he contributed his creative spirit to, and every last doodle in my high school sketchbooks I have from him, no matter how silly. He inspires me every day to be kind to myself and keep going no matter what. 
Love you tons man. I’m so lucky to have had you in my life.

name: Ajay
text: I never met you, Izzy, but I feel like I know a part of you through your dad. The love and pride he carries for you says so much. Life may be short, but yours feels full of courage and spirit. Your story will stay with me!

name: annemarie maes
text: My son, Amor was friends with Izzy and their rag-tag group of super sweet, creative, caring high schoolers. They would have movie meetups where the kids would watch Eraser Head and eat donuts. I loved listening in on their conversations and cracking up, Izzy had a very sharp sense of humor. I loved that my son had the support of Izzy as a friend, it meant a lot to us.

name: Kim Fellows-Peake
text:
It’s hard to know what to say, as I have so so many great memories of Izzy.  Through their life, they’ve been such a creative, energetic and loving force… it’s been a pleasure to have known them and the world feels like a lesser place without them.  

As a little kid, Izzy gave Matt and I our first real glimpse into the sugar-crazies… The family came over with cupcakes for July 4th, of which Izzy ate two. We thought nothing of it, but literally 30 minutes later they were standing on the coffee table with arms out wide, spinning like a helicopter and screaming in joy.  An hour later, we were on the baseball diamond at the park and Izzy’s at-bat was tears and screaming.  The sugar wore off and we were all hooped.

A year or two later we were at the Penston’s for a pizza night and Izzy was wearing a hello kitty t-shirt, a kitchen apron, kids underpants, tall striped tube socks, and heart shaped sunglasses.  It was a pretty special look.  When I looked towards Zoe, she just said “Izzy dressed themselves today”.  They were so proud of that amazing outfit.

Their creativity and enthusiasm was seeded early and continued throughout their life.  We have so many happy memories of the infamous “sabotage” story when Izzy and Wendy were left without a babysitter for the first time, the stunning pics of Izzy dressed up for cosplay events, the plays at the Alameda Point theatre, the rock-tumbling and the frequent refrain of “are the rocks shiny yet.”  

As Matt shared, we loved to be able to make Izzy laugh.  On our visits over the last few years, they were kind enough to hang out with us old-folks while we chatted, told stories, and played Rummikub at the kitchen table.  The best was when we could catch them off-guard with some story or joke and make them crack up.  Or catch them giggling when Frank would jump on their lap and get in the way while Zoe was trying to deliver their meds.  

You will be missed, Izzy.  Love you tons.

name: Julie-Ann
text:
This is such a beautiful tribute! I have always been in awe of Izzy and the way they faced the difficult hand they were given. They lived their life with such passion, curiosity, humor, creativity, thoughtfulness, love and strength.

In one of Izzy’s many interviews, they said, “People say I am an inspiration, and I just don’t get it. I’ve always lived like this, and I am just making the best of a bad situation, like literally everyone else.”

Oh Izzy, you truly shined, and you continue to shine as a bright light of inspiration. Your strength and determination in the fight against FA were extraordinary, and so is the fierce love, strength, and courage your whole family shows every single day.

I feel so grateful to have known Izzy. My memories of them will always spark inspiration, and remind me of how to be better, kinder, gentler, and braver in the world.

name: Matt Peake
text:
When Izzy became a teenager, I enjoyed the challenge of making them laugh. There was a lot of “you wouldn’t believe what your father did at work this week” jokes because they always delivered. 
They were so smart, witty, and special. I consider myself remarkably fortunate to have spent so much time with them over the years.

name: Zoe Penston
text: Izzy, you made me endlessly proud every day. I love you so much and I will be forever grateful to have been your mom.
